comments.text: Borneo is a beautiful island, but it's not some undiscovered paradise, it's a major tourist destination and the coastline is covered in litter (plastic bottles / industrial fishing rubbish) that no one ever cleans up.

comments.author: @radianceTLC
comments.text: Beautiful place indeed  but Sarawak on the north with its rainforest timber and land totally sold by ONE person, Taib Mahmud in 40 years. The Punans retreating into Kalimantan. Sabah the same, Now Indonesia’s new capital, Nusantara, (the present President bought half of the land there to build this capital) is clearing the rainforest again. How much more to go! The local indigenous people have no say. Beautiful may be, but how long with corruption and greed??

comments.author: @harry55t
comments.text: Thank you for showing my homeland to the world. I was born in (the then) very small trading town of Sibu, on the mighty Rejang River (2600 km long) way back in 1955. Yes. I am an old man now. Thank you so much.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: It is an honor to feature such a beautiful homeland, and I’m so glad this video brought back those special memories of Sibu for you.

comments.author: @TopUnrealPlanet
comments.text: This video honestly feels like a hidden masterpiece. The pacing, the visuals, and the emotional tone blend together so perfectly that the experience feels almost hypnotic. It quietly builds curiosity scene after scene until you’re completely immersed in the mystery. Very few creators understand how to create this kind of atmosphere. If anyone here enjoys this style of deep exploration, we recently uploaded a journey on our channel that continues in that same spirit but uncovers even more unexpected and fascinating secrets.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: I'm so glad you enjoyed the video and felt that deep immersion we were aiming for! That's high praise, and it means a lot.

comments.author: @TopUnrealPlanet4K
comments.text: This video is absolutely mesmerizing from beginning to end. The visuals are stunning, but it’s the emotional storytelling and immersive pacing that truly elevate it into something unforgettable. It quietly pulls you into another reality and leaves you thinking about it long after it’s over. That kind of impact is incredibly rare on YouTube. If that same feeling of curiosity is still lingering for anyone else here, we just uploaded a video that explores a similarly fascinating world but uncovers even deeper and more unbelievable mysteries.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: Thank you so much for such a thoughtful and detailed comment! It means a lot to hear that the emotional storytelling and immersive pacing resonated so deeply with you.

comments.author: @WorldDiscoveries11
comments.text: What an amazing look into life in Borneo. The scenery is breathtaking, and the storytelling made me feel like I was there.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: Thank you so much for your kind words! It means a lot to know that my storytelling resonated with you and brought Borneo to life.

comments.author: @Shrikantexplorer
comments.text: Wow! What a beautiful and inspiring video. The stunning rainforest landscapes, flowing rivers, and authentic glimpse into daily life in Borneo were absolutely captivating. It was fascinating to see how communities remain deeply connected to nature, with the river serving as the heart of transportation, trade, and everyday living. The spirit of cooperation, family values, and communal life in the longhouses is truly admirable and offers an important lesson for the modern world. Thank you for showcasing the resilience, culture, and traditions of the people of Borneo with such respect and authenticity. Looking forward to more amazing journeys and stories from your channel!

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: Thank you so much for such a thoughtful and insightful comment! Your appreciation for the details and the message behind the video truly means a lot to me.
